differential_diagnoses: Septic shock due to bacterial translocation in cirrhosis
Disseminated intravascular coagulation secondary to severe infection
next_steps: Perform dengue virus NS1 antigen and IgM/IgG serology
Submit peripheral blood smear for thick and thin assessment to rule out malaria
Send serum and urine for leptospirosis PCR or microscopic agglutination testing
Obtain echocardiogram to characterize biventricular dysfunction and rule out tamponade
Consult infectious disease specialist regarding travel-acquired tropical infections
Initiate broad-spectrum antimicrobial coverage for potential leptospirosis (e.g., ceftriaxone or doxycycline)
Continue aggressive hemodynamic support with close monitoring of fluid balance to prevent pulmonary edema
